medical_history.history_of_present_illness: ___ year-old mixed-handed man with glioma, at least grade 2 diagnosed ___ when he presented with seizures s/p 12 cycles of temozolomide. Patient developed cramps in the 'L hand' noticed by his mother. Continuous seizures reported today. Non-communicative during seizures, flexing R elbow, twitching R ankle, turning head to the left. No seizures reported since his diagnosis until now. Doses of Keppra were missed on day of presentation. Reported dyspnea on exertion for 4 months, no associated nausea or leg swelling, stable symptoms without deterioration.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Right great toenail removal s/p infection, wisdom teeth removed, Grade 2 glioma diagnosed in ___ when he developed seizures s/p bx and 12 cycles of temozolomide, seizures secondary to left frontal glioma.
medical_history.family_history: Mother, age ___, with hypothyroidism. Father, age ___, with asthma. Four healthy brothers. No family history of brain tumors or neurological conditions.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals - T97.8, BP 114/68, HR 70, RR 16, O2 Sat 98% RA. Patient was sleeping but arousable, oriented x3.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neuro: II-XII largely intact, mild weakness noted on the right > left on evaluation of strength in upper/lower extremities; + dysmetria on the right, unsteady gait.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Subtle area of increased hypodensity in the left posterior frontal lobe, consistent with the patient's known glioma. No evidence of acute intracranial process.
Normal chest x-ray without evidence of pneumonia.
Stable appearance of the lesion in the left frontoparietal region without enhancement. No evidence of acute hemorrhage or areas of abnormal enhancement. No midline shift or hydrocephalus.

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient has a known history of grade 2 glioma diagnosed when he developed seizures. The current presentation of seizures is consistent with his ongoing condition, particularly as he mistakenly missed doses of his anti-seizure medication (keppra), which likely contributed to the seizure activity.
